2605.29612.title: CONCAT: Consensus- and Confidence-Driven Ad Hoc Teaming for Efficient LLM-Based Multi-Agent Systems
2605.29612.paper_summary: Although large language model (LLM) based multi-agent systems (MAS) show their capability to solve complex tasks and achieve higher performance over single agent systems, they lead to huge computational overheads because of heavy communication between agents. Previous research has made efforts to train a sparse multi-agent graph or fine-tune a planner to orchestrate the workflow better. However, such extra training processes introduce computational costs and limit MAS to specific domains, therefore compromising their generalizability. In this paper, we propose CONCAT, a training-free multi-agent collaboration framework based on CONsensus and Confidence-driven Ad hoc Teaming to efficiently organize agent interactions. Specifically, agents are clustered based on their initial answers, and leaders of each cluster are selected based on the agents' confidence. Then, a heuristic function based on the Theory of Mind is designed to predict the collaboration benefits between every two leaders according to their answers and confidence. Finally, an ad hoc multi-agent network is organized after evicting a percentage of communications based on the predicted benefits. Experiments across three LLMs and three benchmarks show that CONCAT achieves up to 2.02x higher efficiency (accuracy/latency ratio) than LLM-Debate and outperforms training-aware methods such as AgentDropout, while reducing average latency by 50.1% on Qwen2.5-14B-Instruct, without any task-specific training.
2605.29612.paper_summary_zh: 儘管基於大型語言模型 (LLM) 的多代理系統 (MAS) 展現了解決複雜任務的能力並在性能上超越單一代理系統，但由於代理之間的重通信，它們導致了巨大的計算開銷。以往的研究已經努力訓練稀疏的多代理圖或微調計劃者，以更好地協調工作流程。然而，這些額外的訓練過程引入了計算成本，並將 MAS 限制在特定領域，從而妨礙了它們的通用性。在本文中，我們提出了 CONCAT，一個基於共識和信心驅動的臨時團隊的無需訓練的多代理協作框架，以有效組織代理互動。具體而言，代理根據其初始答案進行聚類，並根據代理的信心選擇每個聚類的領導者。然後，根據他們的答案和信心，設計一個基於心智理論的啟發式函數來預測每兩位領導者之間的協作收益。最後，在根據預測的收益驅逐一定比例的通信後，組織了一個臨時多代理網絡。在三個 LLM 和三個基準測試中的實驗顯示，CONCAT 的效率（準確性/延遲比）比 LLM-Debate 高出最多 2.02 倍，並且在 Qwen2.5-14B-Instruct 上的平均延遲降低了 50.1%，而無需任何特定任務的訓練。
2605.29612.author: Ziyang Ma et.al.
2605.29612.authors: Ziyang Ma, Dingyi Zhang, Sichu Liang, Jiajia Chu, Pengfei Xia, Hui Zang, Deyu Zhou
